name: Plum Pudding with Grand Marnier Hard Sauce
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Light brown sugar, firmly packed
1 Granny Smith apple, cored and finely chopped
recipeInstructions:
-----------------GRAND MARNIER HARD SAUCE-----------------
1. In a large bowl, combine the butter (at room temperature), powdered sugar, Grand Marnier, and egg yolk.
2. Mix until light and fluffy, about one minute.
3. Stir in more Grand Marnier to taste.
-------------
1. Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C).
2. Butter a 2 1/2-quart pudding mold or crock.
3. Line the mold with waxed paper and butter paper.
4. In the bowl of ingredients, mix together walnuts, currants, raisins, candied orange peel, lemon peel, citron, flour, salt, allspice, and ginger.
5. Add breadcrumbs, brown sugar, eggs, suet, apple, carrot, orange juice, and Grand Marnier.
6. Mix well until everything is thoroughly combined.
7. Pack the mixture tightly into the prepared mold.
8. Cover with a sheet of parchment or waxed paper cut to fit the top.
9. Place the mold in a roasting pan and add enough boiling water to reach halfway up the sides of the mold.
10. Cover the roasting pan and bake for five hours, adding more boiling water as necessary to maintain the water level.
11. Remove the mold from the pan and lift off the paper.
12. Pour Cognac over the pudding and replace the paper.
13. Cover tightly with foil and cool to room temperature.
14. Refrigerate at least three days to allow flavors to mellow (up to three months).
Serving:
--------
1. Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C).
2. Set the mold in the roasting pan; discard the foil.
3. Add enough boiling water to reach halfway up the sides of the mold.
4. Cover with the roasting pan and bake until hot, about two hours.
5. Discard the paper and invert the pudding onto a platter.
6. Cut into wedges to serve.
7. Pass the sauce separately.
Grand Marnier Hard Sauce:
- Cream butter and sugar in a processor until light and fluffy (about one minute).
- Add Grand Marnier and egg yolk, processing until smooth.
- Transfer to a serving bowl and refrigerate until firm.
- Bring to room temperature before serving.
